title: eupatorium subintegerrimum var. aliciae
intro: Eupatorium subintegerrimum var. aliciae, also known as Alicia's Joe Pye weed, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, Alicia's Joe Pye weed has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. This plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing relief from pain and inflammation. Alicia's Joe Pye weed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves can also be dried or used fresh in various herbal remedies.
